title: The Mage
timelinePosition: Opens on the exact beat where Chapter 1 ends.
openLocation: Farstrider border post at Thalassian Pass.
transport: On foot inside the ward circle at open; southbound on Thalassian horses once Sylvanas lets her out.
immediateObjective: Jaina must get Sylvanas to act on the contamination before more time is lost.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina perception only.
No thaw, trust, or easy camaraderie between Jaina and Sylvanas yet.
No grain answer or organized Cult infrastructure implied in this chapter.

povKnowsAtOpen: The leylines south of the border are contaminated by something necromantic.
She can identify the signature as broadly death-aligned and necromantic, even if the banned discipline itself sits outside her formal training.
She crossed into Quel'Thalas while tracking it.
The Kirin Tor did not authorize this trip and Antonidas does not know where she is in real time.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Sylvanas already knows who she is.
Whether the elves will release her or keep her as a prisoner and a problem.
What the contamination is doing on the physical ground beyond the leylines.
The exact magical step that turns living contamination into post-death behavior.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No trust between Jaina and Sylvanas.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Sylvanas is convinced enough to release Jaina and ride south herself.
The contamination is physical, lethal, and big enough to swallow the whole region, not just a strange magical disturbance.
Cyndia, Lyriel, Neral, and one unnamed escort ranger are now part of the riding party heading toward Millhaven.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina can explain the contamination clearly enough, any sensible person should act. Sylvanas looks like one more hard soldier standing between the truth and the road south.
pov.surfaceObjective: Get out of the ward circle and get moving before the contamination outruns her again.
pov.pressureSource: The only way south runs through Sylvanas, who has the room, the horses, the escort, and every reason to treat Jaina like a liar or a fool.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps talking smaller than she really thinks, as if a soldier needs the softened version, and keeps expecting being correct to carry the whole argument.
pov.chapterTest: Sylvanas understands more than Jaina gives her credit for, then proves on the road and in the fight that field judgment is a kind of intelligence Jaina has been discounting.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep treating Sylvanas like an obstacle she has to drag toward the truth, or start treating her as the other half of a problem Jaina cannot solve alone.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina still hates the custody and still does not trust Sylvanas, but she stops reading her as a blunt instrument. By the end of the ride, the Ranger-General's read matters to her whether she likes it or not.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 3 opens with Jaina more willing to speak in her full register and more aware that surviving this road depends on skills she does not have.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The woman with the room, the horses, the escort, and the power to decide whether this becomes action or another dead end.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: A cold military obstructionist — dangerous, precise, and fundamentally uninterested in the investigation except as a custody problem.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Sylvanas listens fast, reads the confiscated map, spots the weak points in Jaina's story without missing the strong ones, and handles a real fight with fast, practical control Jaina has never had to think about.
throughPov.interactionRule: Jaina starts out too careful and too translated-down. After the register correction she slips back into her real mage language — still tense, still annoyed, but no longer talking to Sylvanas like she is slow.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Prisoner and custodian. At best, release on a leash.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The custody arrangement turns into a working investigation ride, and Jaina's read plus Sylvanas's field judgment start functioning together instead of at cross purposes.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Jaina can think Sylvanas's name easily enough; the distance shows up in tone instead. She starts formal, irritated, and a little superior. The superiority takes a hit before the chapter ends.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No trust, no easy camaraderie, no sudden friendship. This chapter only opens the first real crack in Jaina's read of Sylvanas.

entries.rule: Leads the investigation south once she decides to test Jaina's claim.
Border-post officer who transitions into active escort lead. The border post is left to her deputy once she rides out.
Right-flank escort. Best shot in the mounted detail.
Third named escort. Strongest rider in the group if someone has to be sent north later.
Fourth saddle only; do not build later continuity around her.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 3 opens with the same party approaching Millhaven. All three named escort elves are still present at Chapter 2 close.

scenes.title: The Interrogation
scenes.sceneFunction: Move Jaina from prisoner to escorted guide, and let her be wrong about Sylvanas in public.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Sylvanas knows only the border breach and Jaina's identity.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina has to convince the one person with the power to move, and her habit of explaining downward starts hurting her case.

enrichment.detail: Canon Warcraft III places Jaina at twenty-three, already Antonidas's standout apprentice and a frontline plague investigator; that supports the speed, authority, and frayed impatience she brings to the interrogation even in this story's deliberate solo-border divergence.

beats.goal: Make Sylvanas listen before more time is lost.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas's authority turns two words into a deadline, not an invitation.

outcome.summary: Jaina finally gets a real listener, but Sylvanas is judging her as hard as she is listening.

newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas's attention feels different from the officers Jaina already failed to move.
Jaina opens with the contamination, not apology or diplomacy.
sensoryAnchors: ward pressure on Jaina's magic
Sylvanas's shadow across the circle
roughness in Jaina's voice after hours of repetition
beats.transitionIntent: question-pressure: Jaina pushes the warning as far as it will go before Sylvanas decides what to do with her.

beats.goal: Explain the leyline corruption clearly enough to justify action.
beats.conflict: Jaina has to turn a hard magical reading into words a soldier can use, and she makes the mistake of talking smaller than she really thinks.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas hears her out, but Jaina is still a problem that may happen to be useful.

beats.valueShift: dangerous opening -> someone is finally listening
newOnPageInformation: The contamination reads necromantic, not standard arcane corruption.
Jaina knows the broad forbidden signature well enough to name the school even though she has never been trained in its applied practice.
Jaina followed that death-tainted wrongness north until the Thalassian ward-stones began carrying the same damage, and she crossed the border trying to confirm the read.
Her map and tools were confiscated at the Pass.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina is exaggerating the danger.
Whether the contamination is local or regional.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Jaina starts plain and practical, naming corrupted leylines, ward-stone spread, and urgency before one slip into sharper mage-register precision reminds everyone how fast she actually thinks.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's hands tracing invisible leyline lines
frost burns on her knuckles
Cyndia hearing the same explanation land differently this time
beats.transitionIntent: weak-point pressure: Sylvanas goes straight for the softest part of the story — why Jaina came alone.

beats.goal: Survive the hard questions without losing the case.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas drills the exact weak point: no credentials, no delegation, no Kirin Tor backing, no real authority except Jaina's own certainty.

outcome.summary: Jaina admits she came alone because nobody believed her, which makes her argument more vulnerable at the same moment it becomes more honest.

newOnPageInformation: The Kirin Tor did not send Jaina.
Antonidas does not know where she is except for a note she left behind.
Being brilliant has also left Jaina very alone in this.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas sees isolation as proof of obsession or proof of urgency.
sensoryAnchors: silence after Jaina admits she left only a note
ward-stone hum filling the gap
Jaina trying not to sound embarrassed by the truth
beats.transitionIntent: decision-pressure: Sylvanas has enough to decide whether Jaina leaves the circle as a guide or stays in it as a problem.

beats.dilemma: Jaina cannot make Sylvanas believe the theory by argument alone.
beats.decision: She will prove it on the road.
newOnPageInformation: Once Sylvanas sees a test worth taking, she moves fast.
Jaina's best chance is no longer persuasion but demonstration.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the road will prove Jaina right or make her look mad.
sensoryAnchors: Sylvanas's hand at the ward edge
the almost-audible click of her decision locking in
beats.transitionIntent: immediate-action: conditional release follows so fast Jaina barely has time to feel relieved.

beats.goal: Get out of the circle and move south.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas gives terms: escort, watched hands, no freedom to disappear off the road; Jaina accepts because every lost minute matters more than pride.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets movement, but only under armed custody.

newOnPageInformation: Jaina says out loud what she cannot do: she can read the lines, but she cannot keep herself alive on the road alone.
Sylvanas hears usefulness in the admission.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How the escort thinks of her: guide, prisoner, or both.
sensoryAnchors: barrier dropping when Cyndia pulls the anchor stone
raw leyline noise flooding back into Jaina's senses
stiff legs after hours on stone
beats.transitionIntent: urgency-spike: once Jaina has her magic back, the need to re-check the contamination hits at full force.

beats.goal: Re-check the readings with her returned staff and get moving.
beats.conflict: The contamination is worse than it was eight hours earlier.

outcome.summary: Release comes too late to feel like relief because the clock has visibly accelerated.

beats.valueShift: constrained forward motion -> the clock is running
newOnPageInformation: Cyndia rides south with the party.
Lyriel and Neral are named as part of Sylvanas's mounted detail.
Neral is the strongest relay rider in the group if a dispatch must go north later.
sensoryAnchors: staff resonance snapping back through Jaina's grip
horses being brought up
escort formation settling around Sylvanas without discussion
beats.transitionIntent: road-commitment: they leave the Pass immediately and cross into Lordaeron.

scenes.title: The Road to Millhaven
scenes.sceneFunction: Take the warning off the page and put it on the road.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is urgent, but how it is getting into the world is still unknown.
scenes.arcPressure: The road keeps proving that Jaina can read the wrongness but not the field around it, while Sylvanas understands more of both than Jaina wanted to admit.

enrichment.detail: Thalassian Pass is the warded southern bottleneck into Quel'Thalas, historically defended by elfgates and Farstrider patrols. Once the party leaves that grid, the magical dampening buffer vanishes and the escort's movement shifts from homeland patrol ease to exposed-border vigilance.

beats.conflict: Once outside the ward network, the corrupted leyline signal slams into Jaina at full strength.

outcome.summary: Jaina has the strongest reading yet, but the severity is almost physically painful.

beats.valueShift: the clock is running -> heading into something larger
newOnPageInformation: The elven wards were dampening what Jaina felt.
The escort moves differently once they leave home ground.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the contamination source is a place, an object, or an ongoing act.
sensoryAnchors: ward-hum cutting off behind them
raw leyline pressure like sudden over-bright light
horses tightening under the riders outside the wards
beats.transitionIntent: test-pressure: Jaina has to check whether the physical world is carrying the same wrongness as the lines.

beats.goal: Confirm whether the land itself is contaminated.
beats.conflict: Jaina kneels in the road and puts her hand to the dirt while the others watch the trees, which shows how little she thinks about being an easy target.

outcome.summary: She proves the wrongness is in the ground and water, but not what put it there.

beats.valueShift: heading into something larger -> this has a physical footprint
newOnPageInformation: The corruption is in the ground, not just in the lines.
Whatever is moving through the land is taking hold inside living systems rather than remaining a surface taint.
Sylvanas and the rangers cover her because Jaina would have stayed kneeling until something bit her.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Why some places are worse than others.
sensoryAnchors: packed earth under Jaina's palm
wrong metallic-copper taste in the magic
Lyriel and Neral covering opposite angles while Cyndia watches the tree line
beats.transitionIntent: briefing-pressure: Jaina tries to explain the reading on the move and reveals what she still assumes about Sylvanas.

beats.goal: Explain the leyline theory on the move.
beats.conflict: Jaina slips into the careful, thinned-down explanation she uses when she expects to be misunderstood. Sylvanas cuts across it by showing she read the confiscated map and followed the route just fine.

outcome.summary: Jaina's explanation lands, but her internal model of Sylvanas breaks in the process.

beats.valueShift: this has a physical footprint -> I was wrong about her
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas read Jaina's map closely enough to understand where the trouble gathers.
Millhaven is the first place where all the lines pinch together.
Jaina's first instinct was to undersell the explanation, and she realizes it too late.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the village is source, casualty, or both.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Let the correction happen mid-ride. Once Sylvanas proves she followed the map, Jaina stops translating downward and starts talking in her natural mage cadence — longer, sharper, more exact.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's hands stopping in mid-gesture
Sylvanas's utterly unembarrassed correction
horse tack and road rhythm continuing around the awkward pause
beats.transitionIntent: world-interruption: the land gets too visibly wrong for the conversation to stay theoretical.

beats.conflict: The land grows visibly wrong even before they see the village clearly.

outcome.summary: They are on the right path, and the evidence is already worse than Jaina hoped.

beats.valueShift: I was wrong about her -> this is real in a way I was not ready for
newOnPageInformation: Empty farms and cold chimneys tell them they are riding into the worst patch yet.
Whatever is happening is reaching kitchens and hearths, not just wards and stones.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What happened to the people of Millhaven.
sensoryAnchors: cold chimneys
rotting crops
staff crystal reacting without Jaina's command
beats.transitionIntent: kinetic-escalation: the road horror turns from environmental evidence into an attack before they can reach the village.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beats 7-10 run four consecutive scene beats without a sequel. The pressure works, but the rhythm stays intentionally scene-heavy until the wildlife attack supplies the next breathing turn.

scenes.title: The Wildlife Attack
scenes.sceneFunction: Make the plague physical and ugly, and force Jaina to watch Sylvanas fight in real time.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The land is contaminated, but the full biological effect is not yet visible.
scenes.arcPressure: Theory turns into bodies and teeth. Jaina has to watch Sylvanas command a real fight, and Sylvanas has to make room for Jaina's magic instead of just hauling a prisoner along.

enrichment.detail: Warcraft plague lore treats infected bodies as crossing from sickness into feral, answerable undeath shortly after death. This scene should stop just short of full reanimation disclosure and instead sell the horrifying threshold where living tissue is already answering necromancy.

beats.conflict: Two sick deer burst out of the trees wild-eyed and half-ruined, and wolves hit the horses from the side before the first shock has passed.

outcome.summary: The rangers drop the deer, but the wolves force Jaina to cast on instinct and one deer keeps moving after it should be dead.

beats.valueShift: this is real in a way I was not ready for -> the contamination has teeth
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can cast fast when fear burns the hesitation out of her.
Sylvanas sees the whole mess at once — where the horses are, where the wolves are, who has a clear shot — and moves people with almost no words.
The contamination can push living bodies past natural death.
Cyndia is the decisive finishing shot under pressure.
Lyriel is first to loose on the moving target.
Neral is steady enough under panic to keep the horses controlled.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this is one cursed stretch of road or the rule for the whole region.
sensoryAnchors: filmed deer eyes
wolves hitting from the flank too fast for comfort
antlers scoring dirt
Cyndia's second arrow through the skull
beats.transitionIntent: diagnostic-pressure: Jaina has to put her hands on the carcass and decide how far the contamination has progressed.

beats.dilemma: Write this off as one cursed stretch of road, or admit the whole countryside may be sick.
beats.decision: Keep moving toward Millhaven because the source answers are still ahead.
beats.valueShift: the contamination has teeth -> forward into the epicenter
newOnPageInformation: The necromantic agent is in tissue, blood, and organs.
The contamination is entering living creatures through the environment.
The deer's violence began before clean death, which means the necromancy is already active in living flesh rather than being laid on afterward.
Sylvanas waits for Jaina's answer before deciding how bad the road has become, and Jaina notices it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What first carried it into living bodies.
sensoryAnchors: hot wrong pulse in dead flesh
Jaina's hand on the carcass
horses sidestepping the smell
beats.transitionIntent: forward-pull: the party remounts with Jaina's answer still hot in her hands and closes on Millhaven's outer edge.

beats.conflict: The village now reads like a place already emptied by what just hit the deer.

outcome.summary: They have reached the village they were aiming for, and it looks dead from a distance.

beats.valueShift: forward into the epicenter -> Millhaven is wrong before they enter it
newOnPageInformation: Millhaven's hearths are cold.
The village is where the wrongness knots tightest.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anyone is still alive inside.
sensoryAnchors: smoke absence
village outline in fading light
the road narrowing into silence
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-hook: Chapter 3 opens on the same mounted party approaching the village proper.

factsLockedForChapter3: The same mounted group reaches Millhaven.
Cyndia, Lyriel, Neral, and one unnamed escort ranger are all already part of the riding party.
The contamination is physical, lethal, and big enough to swallow the whole region.
How it first got into living bodies is still unknown.
factsNotYetEarned: Anything warmer than grim working alignment between Jaina and Sylvanas.

normalizations: Beat 2 dialogueGuidance was compressed from near-line wording into intent-and-register guidance.
Beat 12 transitionIntent now pulls into beat 13 instead of skipping ahead to the chapter boundary.
warnings: Scene 2 beats 7-10 run four consecutive scene beats without a sequel. The escalation is strong, but the chapter stays intentionally scene-heavy until the wildlife attack supplies the next emotional turn.
